title: After-Action Report — Orphan name pools → nations, and a league-wide name scrub
scope: Promoted 5 orphan name pools to full nations with regions and teams; backfilled female names; extended scrubbing to 2,370 junk tokens across ~90 name buckets

rationale.item: Serbian name files existed but there's no Serbia in the game
Five curated national name pools were wired to no region and no team
European/African/Latin buckets riddled with football/basketball club names and sports terms

delegation_events.decision: Promote Serbia, Albania, Estonia, Latvia, Georgia to full nations
delegation_events.quote: Cross-referencing every name bucket against both regions and teams surfaced exactly five buckets with full curated name sets but neither a region nor a team

delegation_events.decision: Backfill Albanian and Georgian female given names
delegation_events.quote: The user asked for both genders

delegation_events.decision: Scope scrubbing to remove only clear junk, keep legit diaspora names
delegation_events.quote: Removed: soccer/basketball/MLS/NBA club names, sports terms in many languages, league/federation/sponsor words, and cross-culture place names sitting in the wrong bucket. Kept: legit place-derived given names (Victoria, Paris, Milan, Sofia, Rosario, Wellington)

actions.description: Added Serbia, Albania, Estonia, Latvia, Georgia as flat regions in regions.json
Added 5 new Frontier Cup teams in youth.py for promoted nations
Backfilled Albanian female given names (~32 names)
Backfilled Georgian female given names (~32 names)
Scrubbed surnames.json removing 1,167 junk tokens across 55 buckets
Scrubbed male_first.json removing 889 junk tokens across 69 buckets
Scrubbed female_first.json removing 310 junk tokens across 46 buckets
Added Iceland with patronymic surname bucket and Frontier team

risk_posture: Subagent cleanup is heuristic across multiple languages; some cross-culture given names may have been incorrectly removed
Scrubber extension uses exact-token matching only to avoid stripping legitimate names containing junk substrings
Second pass cleanup was manual and heuristic; some borderline toponyms retained by design

validation_claims.claim: All three name files + regions.json parse cleanly; no bucket emptied
validation_claims.check_method: Parse JSON and verify bucket references

validation_claims.claim: tests/test_name_regions.py: every region resolves, no Player N fallbacks
Five new countries generate coherent male and female names
validation_claims.check_method: Spot-check name generation for new nations

non_actions.reason: These are shared/legacy pools, not countries
Those countries already exist as teams; regions just draw from shared pool
Had only given names, no surnames; subsequently added in follow-up
Dead data corresponding to countries already in game; out of scope

non_actions.item: Add new nations to presets or international league name-mixes
non_actions.reason: Existing promoted micro-nations (Croatia, Slovenia, Lithuania) also wired this way

residual_risks: Subagent cleanup heuristic; misfiled cross-culture given names (diaspora footballers) may remain
Scrubber protection is exact-token only; could miss substring-based junk if future scrapes embed junk within names
open_hooks: Second-pass residual place/club surname removal (6 toponyms removed manually post-scrub)
Extend scrub_name_pools.py with SCRAPED_SPORTS_JUNK guard for forward protection
Add Iceland with patronymic surname bucket
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Orphan name pools → nations, and a league-wide name scrub
What I deliberately did NOT promote, and why
What I did NOT change (honest caveats)
parser_notes: Comprehensive AAR with clear separation of orphan-pool promotion (design decision) vs league-wide scrubbing (maintenance). Honest about heuristic nature of multi-language cleanup. Follow-ups tracked as commits rather than open items.
